prompt: Which manager led the Florida Marlins to a World Series championship victory over the New York Yankees in 2003?
explanation: Jack McKeon took over the Marlins mid-season and led them to a 4–2 series victory against the heavily favored Yankees.

prompt: Who was the manager of the St. Louis Cardinals when they lost the 2013 World Series to the Boston Red Sox?
explanation: Mike Matheny was the manager of the Cardinals during their 4–2 series loss to Boston in 2013.

prompt: Which team did the Boston Red Sox defeat 4–1 to win the 2018 World Series under manager Alex Cora?
explanation: The Boston Red Sox defeated Dave Roberts and the Los Angeles Dodgers 4–1 to win the 2018 title.

prompt: Who managed the Houston Astros during their 2022 World Series championship victory against Philadelphia?
explanation: Dusty Baker won his first World Series ring as a manager when the Astros defeated the Phillies 4–2 in 2022.

prompt: Which team made a dramatic run to the World Series in 2014 as a Wild Card and lost in 7 games to San Francisco?
explanation: The Kansas City Royals, managed by Ned Yost, reached the 2014 World Series but fell 4–3 to the Giants.

prompt: Who was the head manager of the Atlanta Braves during their 2021 World Series title run?
explanation: Brian Snitker managed the Atlanta Braves to a 4–2 series victory over the Houston Astros in 2021.

prompt: Which team did the San Francisco Giants sweep 4–0 to win the 2012 World Series championship?
explanation: Bruce Bochy's San Francisco Giants swept Jim Leyland's Detroit Tigers in the 2012 World Series.

prompt: Who was the manager of the New York Yankees during their 2024 World Series appearance against the Dodgers?
explanation: Aaron Boone was the manager of the Yankees when they lost the 2024 World Series 4–1 to Los Angeles.

prompt: Which team was defeated by the Philadelphia Phillies in the 2008 World Series?
explanation: The Philadelphia Phillies, managed by Charlie Manuel, defeated the Tampa Bay Rays 4–1 in 2008.

prompt: Who managed the Toronto Blue Jays when they reached the World Series in the 2025 season?
explanation: John Schneider was the manager of the Toronto Blue Jays during their 7-game World Series battle in 2025.

statement: The Washington Nationals won the 2019 World Series under manager Dave Martinez.
explanation: Dave Martinez successfully managed the Wild Card Washington Nationals to a 4–3 series victory over Houston in 2019.

statement: Joe Torre managed the New York Yankees to a World Series championship victory in the year 2000 against the New York Mets.
explanation: Joe Torre led the Yankees to a 4–1 victory over Bobby Valentine's Mets in the famous 2000 Subway Series.

statement: The Cleveland Indians were managed by Terry Francona when they lost the 2016 World Series to the Chicago Cubs.
explanation: Terry Francona was the manager of the Indians during their epic 7-game series loss to Joe Maddon's Cubs in 2016.

statement: The St. Louis Cardinals won the 2004 World Series by sweeping the Boston Red Sox 4–0.
explanation: The Boston Red Sox swept the St. Louis Cardinals 4–0 in 2004, not the other way around.

statement: Phil Garner managed the Houston Astros during their 2005 World Series appearance against the Chicago White Sox.
explanation: Phil Garner was the manager when the Astros made their first-ever World Series appearance in 2005, getting swept by the White Sox.

statement: The Texas Rangers won the 2023 World Series by defeating the Arizona Diamondbacks in 6 games (4–2).
explanation: The Texas Rangers defeated the Arizona Diamondbacks 4–1 (5 games total) in the 2023 World Series.

statement: Clint Hurdle managed the Colorado Rockies when they reached the World Series in 2007.
explanation: Clint Hurdle managed the Wild Card Rockies to the World Series in 2007, where they were swept by Boston.

prompt: Order these World Series champions chronologically by their winning year (earliest first).
events: St. Louis Cardinals managed by Tony La Russa (2006)
New York Yankees managed by Joe Girardi (2009)
San Francisco Giants managed by Bruce Bochy (2014)
Houston Astros managed by Dusty Baker (2022)
explanation: Order: St. Louis Cardinals (2006), New York Yankees (2009), San Francisco Giants (2014), Houston Astros (2022).

prompt: Order these managers based on when they appeared on the losing side of a World Series in this table (earliest first).
explanation: Order: Bobby Valentine (2000), Dusty Baker (2002), Jim Leyland (2006), Ron Washington (2010).

prompt: Order these championship series based on the total number of games played to decide the winner (fewest to most).
explanation: Order: 2007 (4 games), 2003 (6 games), 2001 (7 games), 2025 (7 games).
Mike Scioscia led the Anaheim Angels to a thrilling 4–3 series victory over Dusty Baker's Giants.
Terry Francona's Red Sox captured another title by completing a dominant 4–0 sweep of the Rockies.
Tony La Russa's Cardinals won a legendary, back-and-forth 7-game series against Ron Washington's Rangers.
John Farrell coached the Boston Red Sox to a 4–2 series victory over the Cardinals to clinch the title at Fenway Park.
The Atlanta Braves overcame the odds to defeat Dusty Baker's Houston Astros 4–2 in the series.

prompt: Which manager led the Arizona Diamondbacks to their historic 2001 World Series title against the New York Yankees?
explanation: Bob Brenly was the manager of the Arizona Diamondbacks during their dramatic 4–3 series victory in 2001.

prompt: Who was the manager of the Tampa Bay Rays during their 2020 World Series appearance against the Los Angeles Dodgers?
explanation: Kevin Cash managed the Tampa Bay Rays during the 2020 World Series, which was played in a neutral-site bubble environment.

prompt: Which team won the 2006 World Series under manager Tony La Russa by defeating the Detroit Tigers?
explanation: The St. Louis Cardinals defeated the Detroit Tigers 4–1 to capture the 2006 World Series title.

prompt: Who was the manager of the Philadelphia Phillies when they lost the 2009 World Series to the New York Yankees?
explanation: Charlie Manuel managed the Phillies to the Finals in both 2008 (won) and 2009 (lost 4-2 to the Yankees).

prompt: Which team did the San Francisco Giants defeat 4–3 in a thrilling 7-game series to win the 2014 World Series?
explanation: Bruce Bochy's San Francisco Giants edged out Ned Yost's Kansas City Royals in a classic 2014 series finish.

prompt: Who managed the Houston Astros during their 2019 World Series appearance against the Washington Nationals?
explanation: A.J. Hinch was the manager of the Astros during their 7-game World Series defeat to Washington in 2019.

prompt: Which team won the 2007 World Series under the management of Terry Francona?
explanation: Terry Francona led the Boston Red Sox to a 4–0 sweep over the Colorado Rockies to capture the 2007 title.

prompt: Who was the manager of the New York Mets when they lost the 2015 World Series to the Kansas City Royals?
explanation: Terry Collins was the manager of the New York Mets during their 4–1 series loss to Kansas City in 2015.

prompt: Which franchise was defeated by the Texas Rangers in a 4–1 series during the 2023 World Series?
explanation: The Texas Rangers defeated Torey Lovullo's Arizona Diamondbacks 4–1 to win the 2023 championship.

prompt: Who was the manager of the St. Louis Cardinals when they were swept 4–0 by the Boston Red Sox in 2004?
explanation: Tony La Russa was the manager of the Cardinals during their 2004 World Series appearance.

statement: The Chicago Cubs won the 2016 World Series under manager Joe Maddon by defeating the Cleveland Indians.
explanation: Joe Maddon successfully guided the Cubs to a historic 4–3 series comeback victory over Terry Francona's Indians in 2016.

statement: The New York Yankees defeated the Florida Marlins 4–2 to win the 2003 World Series.
explanation: The Florida Marlins defeated the New York Yankees 4–2 to win the 2003 championship under Jack McKeon.

statement: Dave Roberts managed the Los Angeles Dodgers to back-to-back World Series titles in the 2024 and 2025 seasons.
explanation: Dave Roberts successfully led the Dodgers to consecutive titles, defeating the Yankees in 2024 and the Blue Jays in 2025.

statement: The Detroit Tigers were managed by Jim Leyland when they were swept 4–0 by the San Francisco Giants in 2012.
explanation: Jim Leyland's Tigers reached the World Series in 2012 but were swept in 4 games by Bruce Bochy's Giants.

statement: The Anaheim Angels won the 2002 World Series as a Wild Card team under manager Mike Scioscia.
explanation: Mike Scioscia led the Wild Card Angels to a 4–3 series victory over the San Francisco Giants in 2002.

statement: Rob Thomson managed the Philadelphia Phillies to a World Series championship victory in 2022.
explanation: Rob Thomson's Phillies lost the 2022 World Series 4–2 to Dusty Baker's Houston Astros.

statement: The Houston Astros defeated the Chicago White Sox 4–0 to win the 2005 World Series.
explanation: The Chicago White Sox swept the Houston Astros 4–0 in the 2005 World Series under manager Ozzie Guillén.

prompt: Order these World Series runners-up chronologically by the year they reached the Fall Classic (earliest first).
explanation: Order: San Francisco Giants (2002), Detroit Tigers (2006), Texas Rangers (2011), Toronto Blue Jays (2025).

prompt: Order these managers based on when they won their first World Series title listed in this table (earliest first).
explanation: Order: Joe Torre (2000), Jack McKeon (2003), Bruce Bochy (2010), Brian Snitker (2021).

prompt: Order these championship series based on the total number of games played to decide the winner (fewest to most).
explanation: Order: 2004 (4 games), 2000 (5 games), 2022 (6 games), 2016 (7 games).
Bob Brenly led the Diamondbacks to an epic 4–3 series victory over Joe Torre's Yankees.
Jack McKeon's Marlins captured the title in 6 games against the New York Yankees.
Joe Girardi led the Yankees to a 4–2 series victory over Charlie Manuel's Phillies to claim the title.
Bruce Bochy's Giants won a thrilling 7-game series against the Royals to secure another championship.
Dave Martinez guided the Nationals to a historic 4–3 series victory where the away team won every game.
